reasoning: The informal statement says "a group contains exactly one element of order 2 ⇒ that element is in the center." The Lean theorem encodes "exactly one element of order 2" as the conjunction of existence (`hx : orderOf x = 2`) and uniqueness (`hx1 : ∀ y, orderOf y = 2 → y = x`), which is the standard way to formalize "there is exactly one x with property P, namely this x." The conclusion `x ∈ center G` uses `Subgroup.center`, defined in Mathlib as `{z | ∀ g, g * z = z * g}`, which matches the usual definition of the center. `G` is an arbitrary group with no extra hypotheses (no finiteness, no commutativity, etc.). The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
